goal: Implement Forge Platform v2 Phases 2-4: Database & API, Web UI, AI Features — per docs/FORGE-PLATFORM-V2.md Sections 7-11. Builds on completed Phase 1 (forge-platform-v2-phase1).

tasks.name: context-assembly-budget-improvements
tasks.description: Implement the 4 context assembly improvements from Phase 1 deep-verify: output token reservation, max_pct enforcement, SHOULD guidelines 80% threshold, intra-section partial truncation. Also add system_contract section to SECTION_DEFS and load_global/save_global to StorageAdapter Protocol.
tasks.instruction:
Follow the existing plan in .claude/plans/bright-cuddling-rabin.md which has the full implementation spec. Key changes:
1. Add load_global/save_global to StorageAdapter Protocol in core/storage.py
2. In core/llm/context.py _apply_budget(): output reservation (25% or max_output_tokens), per-section max_pct caps, SHOULD guidelines 80% check
3. Add _split_section_items() and _truncate_section_partial() for intra-section truncation
4. Add system_contract SectionDef (priority=1, max_pct=None, truncatable=False)
5. Add contract parameter to assemble() and _gather_system_contract() method
acceptance_criteria: Output token reservation: subtracts max_output_tokens (or 25% fallback) from context window
Per-section max_pct enforcement: sections capped to their max_pct of available tokens
SHOULD guidelines removed when total context > 80% of window
Intra-section truncation: removes individual items before removing entire section
system_contract section present in SECTION_DEFS with priority=1, truncatable=False
load_global/save_global in StorageAdapter Protocol
Backward compatible — assemble() works without contract parameter

tasks.name: forge-client-abstraction
tasks.description: Create ForgeClient that wraps all Forge operations and routes to JSONBackend (standalone) or APIBackend (remote). Phase 1 item 1.10 that was deferred. This is the foundation for CLI remote mode (T-020).
tasks.instruction:
Create core/client.py with:
1. ForgeConfig dataclass: mode ('standalone'|'local'|'remote'), data_dir, api_url, api_key
2. ForgeClient class with __init__(config) that selects backend
3. JSONBackend — wraps current module functions (pipeline, decisions, changes, etc.)
4. APIBackend — stub class with NotImplementedError (implemented in T-020)
5. Client methods: create_task, get_context, list_projects, add_decision, record_change, etc.
6. Config loading from ~/.forge/config.toml (fallback to standalone mode)

Reference: FORGE-PLATFORM-V2.md Section 8.3
acceptance_criteria: core/client.py exists with ForgeClient class
JSONBackend wraps existing module functions
APIBackend stub exists (raises NotImplementedError)
Config loading from ~/.forge/config.toml or defaults to standalone
ForgeClient selects backend based on config.mode

tasks.name: database-schema-design
tasks.description: Design and implement SQL migration scripts for the full Forge database schema: all core entity tables, junction tables for relationships, indexes (GIN for arrays, FTS for knowledge). Based on Section 7.4.
tasks.instruction:
Create forge-api/migrations/ directory with:
1. 001_initial_schema.sql — all tables from Section 7.4: projects, objectives, key_results, ideas, tasks, decisions, changes, lessons, guidelines, knowledge, knowledge_versions, ac_templates
2. 002_junction_tables.sql — knowledge_links, task_ac, task_dependencies, task_knowledge, idea_relations
3. 003_indexes.sql — GIN indexes for scopes/tags arrays, FTS index for knowledge, standard indexes for status/foreign keys
4. A simple migration runner script (Python) that applies migrations in order
5. Ensure schema matches entity structures from core/*.py modules exactly (field names, types)

Reference: FORGE-PLATFORM-V2.md Section 7.4
acceptance_criteria: SQL migration files exist and are syntactically valid
All entity tables from Section 7.4 defined
Junction tables for all many-to-many relationships
GIN indexes on array columns (scopes, tags)
FTS index on knowledge (title + content)
Migration runner script applies migrations in order
Schema field names match core/*.py entity structures

tasks.name: postgresql-adapter
tasks.description: Implement PostgreSQLAdapter that satisfies StorageAdapter Protocol. Uses SQLAlchemy or asyncpg for database access. Must pass the same behavioral contract as JSONFileStorage.
tasks.instruction:
Create core/storage_pg.py (or forge-api/storage/postgresql.py) with:
1. PostgreSQLAdapter class implementing StorageAdapter Protocol: load_data, save_data, exists, list_projects, load_global, save_global
2. Connection pool management (asyncpg or SQLAlchemy async)
3. Entity type → table name mapping
4. JSON serialization for JSONB columns
5. Transaction support for atomic operations
6. load_data returns dict in same format as JSONFileStorage (e.g., {'project': ..., 'tasks': [...]})

Key: the adapter translates between row-based DB storage and the dict-based interface that core modules expect. Core modules should work unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: PostgreSQLAdapter implements all 6 StorageAdapter Protocol methods
isinstance check passes: isinstance(pg_adapter, StorageAdapter)
load_data returns dicts in same structure as JSONFileStorage
Connection pooling configured
Handles missing entities (returns default structure, like JSONFileStorage)

tasks.name: knowledge-versioning-postgresql
tasks.description: Implement full knowledge versioning in PostgreSQL: version table with diffs, version history API, rollback support. This is a PostgreSQL-only feature (JSON mode has embedded array versioning).
tasks.instruction:
In the PostgreSQL adapter:
1. knowledge_versions table stores each version separately (version INT, content TEXT, changed_by, change_reason, created_at)
2. On knowledge update: create new version row, increment current_version on knowledge table
3. Diff generation between versions (simple text diff or JSON diff)
4. API to list versions, get specific version, compare versions
5. Rollback: create new version with content from a previous version

Reference: Section 7.2 (JSON = embedded array, PostgreSQL = version table with diff support)
acceptance_criteria: knowledge_versions table populated on every knowledge update
Version history retrievable for any knowledge object
Diff between two versions calculable
Rollback creates new version (not destructive overwrite)
current_version field on knowledge table stays in sync

tasks.name: knowledge-impact-analysis
tasks.description: Implement knowledge impact analysis queries in PostgreSQL. When knowledge is updated, find all entities linked to it and assess potential impact. Uses reverse FK joins for efficiency.
tasks.instruction:
1. Query: given knowledge_id, find all linked entities via knowledge_links table
2. For each linked entity, determine impact based on relation type (required > context > reference)
3. Return structured impact report: affected entity list with impact level
4. In JSON mode: full file scan (acceptable for small projects)
5. Impact detection triggered on knowledge update (can be event-driven later)

Reference: Section 7.2 (JSON = full scan, PostgreSQL = reverse FK JOIN)
acceptance_criteria: Impact query returns all entities linked to a knowledge object
Impact levels assigned based on relation type
Works in PostgreSQL mode (efficient JOINs)
Structured impact report returned as dict/JSON

tasks.name: json-to-postgresql-migration
tasks.description: Build the forge-migrate tool that imports existing JSON project data into PostgreSQL. Must handle all entity types, preserve IDs, create junction table entries, and import knowledge versions.
tasks.instruction:
Create core/migrate.py (or forge-api/migrate.py) with:
1. Read all JSON files from forge_output/{project}/
2. Validate entity integrity (all references resolve)
3. Insert into PostgreSQL tables, preserving ext_id (original ID like T-001, K-001)
4. Create junction table entries for many-to-many relationships (knowledge_links, task_dependencies, etc.)
5. Import knowledge versions as separate rows in knowledge_versions table
6. Report: entities imported, warnings, skipped items
7. CLI command: forge-migrate import --source ./forge_output/my-project --target postgresql://...

Reference: Section 7.3
acceptance_criteria: Reads all entity JSON files from a project directory
Inserts all entities into correct PostgreSQL tables
Preserves original IDs as ext_id / aliases
Creates junction table entries for relationships
Knowledge versions imported as separate rows
Reports import statistics and any warnings

tasks.name: llm-debug-monitor
tasks.description: Implement a comprehensive LLM Debug Monitor that allows developers to inspect exactly what is sent to the LLM and what comes back. Features: (1) Backend debug middleware that captures full request/response payloads for every LLM call — including assembled context, system/user prompts, provider config, raw response, token usage, latency, contract validation results. (2) Debug session storage with unique session IDs. (3) API endpoints to list/view debug sessions with filtering. (4) Frontend Debug Monitor panel toggleable from the UI — shows real-time LLM call log with expandable request/response details, token counts, timing, validation status. (5) Toggle to enable/disable debug capture (off by default for performance). This enables identifying prompt engineering issues, context assembly bugs, token budget problems, validation failures, and provider errors.
tasks.instruction:
Implement LLM Debug Monitor across backend and frontend:

BACKEND (forge-api):
1. Create app/routers/debug.py with endpoints:
   - POST /projects/{slug}/debug/enable — enable debug capture for project
   - POST /projects/{slug}/debug/disable — disable debug capture
   - GET /projects/{slug}/debug/status — check if enabled
   - GET /projects/{slug}/debug/sessions — list debug sessions (with filters: task_id, contract_id, status, date range)
   - GET /projects/{slug}/debug/sessions/{session_id} — full session detail
   - DELETE /projects/{slug}/debug/sessions — clear debug sessions
2. Create app/debug_capture.py middleware/service:
   - DebugCapture class that wraps LLM provider calls
   - Captures: timestamp, contract_id, provider, model, assembled_context (all sections with token counts), system_prompt, user_prompt, raw_request_config, raw_response, parsed_output, validation_results (pass/fail per rule), token_usage (input/output/total), latency_ms, error (if any)
   - Stores sessions in project storage (debug_sessions entity)
   - Toggle via project-level flag
3. Integrate with execution.py — wrap mock execution to emit debug events
4. Add 'debug.session' event type to EventBus

FRONTEND (forge-web):
1. Create components/debug/DebugMonitor.tsx — main panel component:
   - Toggleable overlay/sidebar accessible from project layout
   - Real-time session list with status indicators
   - Expandable session cards showing: contract, provider, model, timing
   - Request tab: assembled context sections, system prompt, user prompt, config
   - Response tab: raw response, parsed output, validation results
   - Token tab: input/output breakdown, cost estimate, budget usage
   - Timeline view for multi-call sessions
2. Create components/debug/DebugToggle.tsx — enable/disable button
3. Create hooks/useDebugSessions.ts — data fetching hook
4. Add debug toggle to project layout header
5. Add debug API methods to lib/api.ts
6. Add TypeScript types to lib/types.ts
acceptance_criteria: Debug capture can be toggled on/off per project via API and UI
Every LLM call (when enabled) captures: full request payload, full response, token usage, latency, validation results
Debug sessions are persisted and queryable with filters (task, contract, status, date)
Frontend Debug Monitor shows real-time list of LLM calls with expandable details
Request details show: assembled context sections with token counts, system/user prompts, provider config
Response details show: raw response, parsed output, validation pass/fail per rule
Token tab shows: input/output breakdown, cost estimate if available
Debug monitor is toggleable from project layout (not visible when disabled)
Debug capture has minimal performance impact when disabled
Sessions can be cleared/deleted via API and UI
npm run build passes with 0 errors

tasks.name: fix-critical-websocket-bugs

tasks.instruction:

2. Line 48: Replace `exists = storage.exists(slug, 'tracker')` with `exists = await asyncio.to_thread(storage.exists, slug, 'tracker')`
3. Add periodic ping in the polling loop (every 30s): send `{"event": "ping"}` to detect dead connections
4. Line 75: Wrap `pubsub.aclose()` with hasattr check for redis-py version compatibility

tasks.name: fix-ac-template-parameter-injection
tasks.description: Fix critical bug: AC template instantiation uses sequential str.replace() for parameter substitution, allowing parameter values containing {placeholder} syntax to inject new placeholders. Use single-pass regex substitution instead.
tasks.instruction:
Fix in forge-api/app/routers/ac_templates.py lines 144-151:
Replace the sequential str.replace() loop with a single-pass regex substitution:
```python
import re
def _substitute(template, params):
    return re.sub(r'{(w+)}', lambda m: str(params[m.group(1)]) if m.group(1) in params else m.group(0), template)
```
Then check for unresolved placeholders on the result.
acceptance_criteria: Parameter values containing {placeholder} syntax do not get treated as template placeholders
Single-pass regex substitution used instead of sequential str.replace()
Unresolved placeholder detection still works correctly

tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/llm/tool_registry.py with:
   - ToolDef dataclass: name, description, parameters (JSON Schema), required_permission (module, action)
   - ToolRegistry class: register(tool), get_tools(context_type, permissions) -> list[ToolDef], execute(tool_name, args, context) -> dict
2. Define GLOBAL tools (always available):
   - searchEntities(entity_type, query, filters) -> list
   - getEntity(entity_type, entity_id) -> dict
   - listEntities(entity_type, slug?, filters?) -> list
   - getProject(slug) -> dict
3. Define SKILL tools (when context.type == 'skill'):
   - updateSkillContent(skill_id, content) -> updated skill
   - updateSkillMetadata(skill_id, category?, tags?, scopes?) -> updated
   - runSkillLint(skill_id) -> lint results
   - getOtherSkill(skill_id) -> skill content (read-only)
4. Each tool function wraps existing Forge API router logic (reuse, don't duplicate).
5. Tools return structured JSON results.
6. Convert to core/llm/provider.py ToolDefinition format for LLM calls.

   - PermissionEngine class
   - load_permissions(config, project_slug?) -> PermissionSet
   - check(permission_set, module, action) -> bool
   - Actions: 'read', 'write', 'delete'
   - Modules: 'skills', 'tasks', 'objectives', 'ideas', 'knowledge', 'guidelines', 'decisions', 'lessons', 'projects', 'ac_templates'
2. PermissionSet: global_perms merged with project_perms (project overrides global).
3. Default permissions (conservative): read=true for all, write=true for skills only, delete=false for all.
4. Permissions stored in LLM config (from T-100).
5. Integration: Tool Registry (T-101) calls check() before executing write/delete tools.
6. Denied tool calls return structured error: {denied: true, module, action, reason}.
acceptance_criteria: PermissionEngine loads global + project permissions from config

tasks.name: implement-session-manager
tasks.description: Create Session Manager — handles chat conversation persistence in Redis. Each conversation is a session with message history, token tracking, cost estimation. Sessions have 24h TTL and emit events for Debug Console.
tasks.instruction:

   - ChatMessage dataclass: id, role (user/assistant/system/tool), content, tool_calls?, tool_results?, tokens_used?, created_at
   - SessionManager class: create(context) -> session_id, load(id) -> ChatSession, save(session), add_message(session_id, message), delete(id), list_sessions(limit?) -> list
2. Redis storage: key 'chat:session:{session_id}', JSON serialized, TTL 24h.
3. On session creation: emit 'llm.session_started' to EventBus.
4. On message added: update token counters.
5. Cost estimation: use ProviderCapabilities.cost_per_1k_input/output.
6. Add API endpoints: GET /api/v1/llm/sessions, GET /api/v1/llm/sessions/{id}.
acceptance_criteria: Sessions stored in Redis with 24h TTL
Create/load/save/delete operations work

tasks.name: implement-agent-loop
tasks.description: Create the Agent Loop — core multi-turn conversation engine. LLM receives messages + tools, responds with text or tool calls, tool results fed back, loop continues until end_turn. Streaming output emitted as events. This is the brain of LLM integration.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/llm/agent_loop.py with:
   - AgentLoop class
   - async run(provider, messages, tools, permissions, config, on_event) -> CompletionResult
   - on_event callback: StreamEvent (token/tool_call/tool_result/complete/error)
2. Loop logic:
   a. Call provider.stream(messages, config_with_tools)
   b. Collect streaming tokens, emit as events
   c. If stop_reason == 'tool_use': parse tool calls
   d. For each tool call: validate JSON Schema, check permissions, execute via ToolRegistry
   e. Build tool_result message, append to messages
   f. Loop back to (a)
   g. If stop_reason == 'end_turn': emit complete event, return
3. Safety limits: max_iterations=10, max_tokens_total=50k (configurable), timeout=120s per iteration
4. Error handling:
   - Provider error -> emit error, retry once
   - Tool error -> send as tool_result (let LLM recover)
   - Permission denied -> send denial as tool_result
   - Timeout -> emit error, stop
5. Async, supports cancellation.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-turn loop: LLM calls tool -> result fed back -> LLM continues
Streaming tokens emitted as events
Tool calls validated against JSON Schema
Permission checks before write/delete tool execution
Safety limits enforced (max iterations, tokens, timeout)
Tool errors gracefully handled (fed back to LLM)
Loop terminates on end_turn or cancellation
Token usage tracked across iterations

tasks.name: create-chat-router-and-ws-streaming
tasks.description: Create Chat Router — FastAPI endpoints for LLM chat. POST /api/v1/llm/chat accepts message with context, orchestrates full flow (context -> tools -> permissions -> agent loop), streams responses via WebSocket.
tasks.instruction:

   - POST /api/v1/llm/chat: {context: {type, id, slug?}, message, session_id?, model?}
     Response: {session_id, status: 'streaming'}
   - GET /api/v1/llm/providers: list configured providers
   - POST /api/v1/llm/providers/test: test connection
   - GET /api/v1/llm/config: feature flags + permissions
   - PUT /api/v1/llm/config: update config
2. POST /chat orchestration:
   a. Load/create session (SessionManager)
   b. Resolve context: load entity by type+id
   c. Build system prompt with context
   d. Load permissions
   e. Get tools from ToolRegistry
   f. Run AgentLoop with streaming
   g. Emit WS events: chat.token, chat.tool_call, chat.tool_result, chat.complete, chat.error
3. Mount in main.py at /api/v1/llm
4. WS events via EventBus -> broadcast to connected clients
5. Context resolver: type -> entity loader mapping

tasks.name: define-capability-contract-registry
tasks.description: Define static capability contract definitions per scope — TypeScript data listing what operations the AI can perform in each scope, with availability flags for tools that don't exist yet.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-web/lib/capabilities.ts:
   a. Define types:
      interface CapabilityDef {
        id: string;          // e.g., 'list-skills'
        label: string;       // e.g., 'List all skills'
        description: string; // e.g., 'View all available skills'
        action: 'READ' | 'WRITE' | 'DELETE';
        scope: string;       // e.g., 'skills'
        toolName: string | null;  // mapped backend tool name, null if not yet implemented
        available: boolean;  // false if tool doesn't exist yet
      }
   b. Define CAPABILITY_CONTRACTS: Record<string, CapabilityDef[]> with entries for 6 scopes:
      - skills: list (READ, listEntities), search (READ, searchEntities), create (WRITE, updateSkillContent), edit (WRITE, updateSkillContent), delete (DELETE, null, available:false), lint (READ, runSkillLint), promote (WRITE, null, available:false), export (READ, null, available:false)
      - tasks: list (READ, listEntities), search (READ, searchEntities), show (READ, getEntity), create (WRITE, null, available:false), update (WRITE, null, available:false), complete (WRITE, null, available:false)
      - objectives: list (READ, listEntities), show (READ, getEntity), create (WRITE, null, available:false), update (WRITE, null, available:false)
      - ideas: list (READ, listEntities), show (READ, getEntity), create (WRITE, null, available:false), update (WRITE, null, available:false)
      - decisions: list (READ, listEntities), show (READ, getEntity), create (WRITE, null, available:false), update (WRITE, null, available:false)
      - knowledge: list (READ, listEntities), show (READ, getEntity), create (WRITE, null, available:false), update (WRITE, null, available:false)
   c. Export helper function getCapabilitiesForScopes(scopes: string[]): CapabilityDef[] — returns merged capabilities for all given scopes (deduped by id)
   d. Export helper function getPermissionStatus(capability: CapabilityDef, permissions: LLMPermissions): 'enabled' | 'no-permission' | 'coming-soon'
      - If !capability.available: return 'coming-soon'
      - If permission for scope+action is false: return 'no-permission'
      - Else: return 'enabled'

tasks.name: git-sync-service
tasks.description: GitSyncService do zarzadzania git clone forge-skills.git. Operacje: init_or_clone, pull (fetch+reset --hard), push (add synced+commit+push), status. Graceful degradation bez git.
tasks.instruction:
1. Utworz forge-api/app/services/git_sync.py.
2. Konstruktor: skills_dir, remote_url (env FORGE_SKILLS_REPO_URL).
3. init_or_clone(): clone lub verify .git.
4. pull(): git fetch && git reset --hard origin/main, then resync_index().
5. push(message): git add tylko sync:true skills, commit, push.
6. status(): git status --porcelain + rev-list HEAD..origin/main.
7. asyncio.to_thread(subprocess.run, ...).
8. Graceful: brak git/repo -> error, nie crash. Brak FORGE_SKILLS_REPO_URL -> 503.
acceptance_criteria: init_or_clone() klonuje lub weryfikuje repo
pull() robi fetch+reset, potem resync indeksu
push() commituje tylko sync:true skills
status() zwraca local changes i behind/ahead
Graceful degradation gdy git brak
